title: Graphic Designers
description: Design or create graphics to meet specific commercial or promotional needs, such as packaging, displays, or logos. May use a variety of mediums to achieve artistic or decorative effects.

tasks.text: Key information into computer equipment to create layouts for client or supervisor.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review final layouts and suggest improvements, as needed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Determine size and arrangement of illustrative material and copy, and select style and size of type.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop graphics and layouts for product illustrations, company logos, and Web sites.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Create designs, concepts, and sample layouts, based on knowledge of layout principles and esthetic design concepts.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Use computer software to generate new images.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with clients to discuss and determine layout design.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Research the target audience of projects.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Draw and print charts, graphs, illustrations, and other artwork, using computer.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mark up, paste, and assemble final layouts to prepare layouts for printer.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Study illustrations and photographs to plan presentation of materials, products, or services.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Maintain archive of images, photos, or previous work products.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare notes and instructions for workers who assemble and prepare final layouts for printing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare illustrations or rough sketches of material, discussing them with clients or supervisors and making necessary changes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Research new software or design concepts.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Produce still and animated graphics for on-air and taped portions of television news broadcasts, using electronic video equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Photograph layouts, using camera, to make layout prints for supervisors or clients.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Write or edit copy for clients.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
